tender_title: Expression of Interest for the Implementation of an End-to-End (E2E) Lending System
tender_ref: CRDB Bank Plc — EOI for E2E Lending System

sections.heading: Company Profile
sections.content:
Isale Investment Limited (formerly Leeds Company Limited) is a Tanzanian-registered ICT services and business consulting firm incorporated on 1st October 2018 (Registration No. 137771411). The company specializes in banking technology solutions, enterprise software implementation, digital transformation, and business strategy consulting.

Isale holds a Payment Service Provider (PSP) licence (NBPSL No. 0000-70) issued by the Bank of Tanzania, valid through 25th August 2030, authorizing the provision of payment system services across Mainland Tanzania and Tanzania Zanzibar. The company is VAT-registered (VRN: 40-313410-P) and holds a current ICT Services business licence from Kinondoni Municipal Council.

The company maintains offices in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania and Dubai, UAE (Le Solerium Towers, Dubai Silicon Oasis), enabling delivery across East Africa and the wider region.

Isale's technology division, Isale Digital, delivers banking solutions, business solutions (ERP, CRM, supply chain management), and government digital transformation solutions. The company's banking solutions practice focuses on digital banking platforms, security architecture, operational efficiency, and secure financial transaction systems.

sections.heading: Understanding of CRDB Bank's Requirements
sections.content:
We understand that CRDB Bank seeks to digitally unify its entire credit lifecycle across Consumer, MSME, and Corporate segments through an integrated End-to-End (E2E) Lending Solution built upon the recently implemented Temenos Transact R21 core banking platform and WSO2 enterprise integration platform.

The Bank requires a modular, integration-led ecosystem that covers:

• Loan Origination and Workflow Layer — customer onboarding, application capture, document management, and process orchestration across all lending segments

• Credit Decisioning Layer — automated and semi-automated credit assessments including eligibility checks, scoring models, policy rules engine, limits management, and multi-level approval workflows integrated with credit bureaus, NIDA, and regulatory databases

• Core Banking Integration — seamless interoperation with Temenos Transact R21 for loan account setup, disbursement, accruals, repayments, restructuring, and financial postings while minimizing CBS customization

• Collateral Management — comprehensive collateral registration, valuation, lien management, and insurance tracking across all asset classes

• Collections and Recovery — automated collections workflows, arrears management, restructuring capabilities, and recovery tracking

• Enterprise Integration — loosely coupled, API-driven integration architecture leveraging WSO2 for secure communication between all lending ecosystem components

• Data Warehouse, Reporting and Analytics — management information, regulatory reporting (Bank of Tanzania), portfolio analytics, and performance dashboards

We further note the requirement for multi-entity support across Tanzania, Burundi, and DRC, segment-specific lending models including Agribusiness and Islamic financing, and an agile phased implementation approach.

sections.heading: Proposed Solution Overview
sections.content:
Isale proposes to implement an integrated E2E Lending Solution leveraging Temenos Infinity (Digital Banking) and Temenos Transact Lending modules, complemented by best-of-breed components where necessary, all orchestrated through the WSO2 integration platform.

Our proposed architecture aligns with CRDB Bank's target state:

• Front-End Layer: Temenos Infinity for digital loan origination across branch, mobile, internet banking, and relationship manager channels — providing a unified customer experience

• Loan Origination System (LOS): Configurable workflow engine supporting multi-segment origination (Consumer, MSME, Corporate, Agribusiness, Islamic) with automated document management, KYC/AML checks, and maker-checker controls

• Credit Decisioning Engine: Rules-based and scorecard-driven credit assessment integrating with Tanzania credit bureaus (CRB Africa, Dun & Bradstreet), NIDA for identity verification, and internal risk models. Supports automated straight-through processing for pre-approved segments and manual intervention workflows for complex credits

• Temenos Transact R21 Integration: Native integration with the existing CBS for product setup, account creation, disbursement, schedule management, accruals, repayment processing, restructuring, and write-off — maintaining Temenos Transact as the system of record

• Collateral Management Module: End-to-end collateral lifecycle management including registration, valuation, perfection tracking, insurance monitoring, and release

• Collections and Recovery: Automated arrears detection, segmented collections strategies, workout management, and recovery tracking with escalation workflows

• WSO2 Enterprise Integration: API management, service orchestration, and event-driven integration patterns connecting all lending components with external systems

• Analytics and Reporting: Real-time dashboards, regulatory reporting (Bank of Tanzania returns), portfolio monitoring, and predictive analytics

sections.heading: Approach and Methodology
sections.content:
We propose an agile, phased implementation methodology designed for incremental value realization and risk-controlled rollout.

Phase 1 — Discovery and Foundation (Weeks 1-6):
• Detailed requirements gathering across Consumer, MSME, and Corporate segments
• Current-state process mapping and gap analysis against target E2E architecture
• Solution design and integration architecture finalization with WSO2 team
• Environment setup including sandbox/development, UAT, and staging
• Project governance framework, RACI matrix, and communication plan
Deliverables: Business Requirements Document, Solution Design Document, Integration Architecture, Project Plan

Phase 2 — Core Lending Build (Weeks 7-18):
• Configure loan origination workflows for Consumer and MSME segments
• Implement credit decisioning rules, scorecards, and approval hierarchies
• Build Temenos Transact R21 integration layer via WSO2 for product setup, disbursement, and repayment processing
• Develop collateral management module
• Unit testing and system integration testing
Stage-Gate: System Integration Test sign-off
Deliverables: Configured LOS, Credit Engine, Collateral Module, SIT Report

Phase 3 — Corporate and Specialized Lending (Weeks 19-28):
• Extend platform for Corporate segment with complex deal structures
• Configure Agribusiness and Islamic financing lending models
• Implement collections and recovery workflows
• Build regulatory reporting and analytics dashboards
• Integration with credit bureaus, NIDA, and external data sources
Stage-Gate: Extended SIT sign-off
Deliverables: Corporate Lending Module, Collections Module, Analytics Layer

Phase 4 — Testing, Training and Go-Live (Weeks 29-36):
• User Acceptance Testing (UAT) with CRDB Bank business teams
• Data migration from legacy lending systems
• Technical training for CRDB IT team (system administration, integration management)
• End-user training using train-the-trainer approach across branches
• Parallel run and production cutover
• Hyper-care support (4 weeks post go-live)
Stage-Gate: UAT sign-off, Go-Live approval
Deliverables: UAT Report, Training Materials, Migration Report, Go-Live Checklist

Phase 5 — Multi-Entity Rollout (Weeks 37-48):
• Configure and deploy to Burundi subsidiary
• Greenfield deployment for DRC subsidiary
• Localization for regulatory requirements per jurisdiction
• Post-deployment optimization and stabilization

Assumptions:
• CRDB Bank will provide a dedicated project team including business SMEs, IT resources, and a Project Sponsor
• Access to Temenos Transact R21 sandbox environment will be provided within 2 weeks of project initiation
• WSO2 platform administration support will be available from CRDB IT team
• Third-party integrations (credit bureaus, NIDA) will require separate commercial agreements managed by CRDB Bank
• Change management and organizational readiness activities will be jointly planned and executed

sections.heading: Team Qualifications
sections.content:
Isale Investment Limited brings together a core team with deep expertise in banking technology, credit risk management, and digital transformation:

• Victor Tesha — Project Director / CEO: MBA (Marketing), BA Social Science. 10+ years in banking across NBC Bank, Azania Bank, UBA Tanzania, and ICB Tanzania. Spearheaded NBC's 2013-2018 strategic turnaround. Expertise in credit and risk management, commercial banking, corporate strategy, product development, and business transformation.

• Prem Ruparelia — Strategic Advisor / Director: MA Corporate Strategy and Governance, MCIPS (UK). 20+ years across procurement, financial services, and consulting in UK, Europe, UAE and East Africa. Blue-chip experience with NatWest Bank, DHL, BAE Systems, Roche Pharmaceuticals. Expertise in investment management, STP, operating model redesign, and large-scale transformation programs.

The team will be augmented with specialist Temenos implementation consultants and WSO2 integration engineers as required for the engagement.

sections.heading: Value Proposition
sections.content:
Isale Investment Limited offers CRDB Bank the following differentiated value:

• Local Presence with International Reach: Headquartered in Dar es Salaam with a Dubai office, we provide on-the-ground delivery with access to regional and international technology talent. As a Bank of Tanzania-licensed PSP, we operate within Tanzania's financial regulatory framework.

• Banking Domain Expertise: Our leadership team brings direct experience from Tanzania's banking sector (NBC, Azania, UBA, ICB), providing deep understanding of local banking practices, credit processes, regulatory requirements, and customer expectations.

• Integration-Led Approach: Our methodology is designed around CRDB Bank's existing technology investments (Temenos Transact R21, WSO2), minimizing CBS customization while maximizing the value of the enterprise integration platform.

• Agile Delivery with Risk Control: Our phased approach delivers incremental business value while maintaining controlled stage-gates, ensuring CRDB Bank has visibility and governance throughout the implementation journey.

• Multi-Entity Capability: With operations spanning Tanzania and Dubai, and understanding of East African banking markets, we are positioned to support CRDB Bank's multi-entity rollout across Tanzania, Burundi, and DRC.
